reasoning: The informal claim "Z/nZ is not a group under multiplication" for n > 1 is logically equivalent to "not every element of Z/nZ has a two-sided multiplicative inverse with respect to 1". This is because associativity and the identity (1 is the unique possible two-sided identity in the multiplicative monoid Z/nZ) are automatically satisfied, so the only group axiom that can fail is the existence of inverses. The Lean statement negates exactly the inverse-existence condition with 1 as the identity, which captures precisely the failure of Z/nZ to be a multiplicative group. The two statements are logically equivalent.
